evals.prompt: Determine the design loads for a 25m simply supported bridge in Uganda with a 7.3m carriageway. The bridge is designed for 25 units of HB loading. The site is in central Uganda with a wind speed of 100 km/h. Using the MoWT Road Design Manual Vol 4, calculate: (1) the HA UDL and KEL, (2) the number of notional lanes, (3) the HB total load, (4) the Combination 1 ULS load factors for HA alone and dead load (concrete), and (5) check whether seismic design is required for this site.
evals.expected_output: HA UDL W = 30.0 kN/m (since L = 25m <= 30m, constant value). KEL = 120 kN per notional lane. 2 notional lanes (7.3m carriageway in 4.6-7.6m range). HB 25 units = 250 kN per axle, total 1000 kN over 4 axles. Combination 1 ULS: HA alone gamma_fL = 1.50, dead concrete gamma_fL = 1.15. Central Uganda is Zone 2 or 3 (A = 0.07 or 0.05), SPC 1 or 2 -- no detailed seismic analysis required but connections must resist 0.20 * dead load reaction. Seismic Section 9 referenced.

assertions.text: HA UDL value is 30.0 kN/m (in range 29-31 kN/m) -- correctly applies constant value for L <= 30m, NOT the formula
Notional lane count is 2 (for 7.3m carriageway in 4.6-7.6m range)
Load factor for HA alone ULS in Combination 1 is in range 1.45-1.55 (should be 1.50)
HB total load is in range 900-1100 kN (25 units * 10 kN/axle * 4 axles = 1000 kN)
Seismic Section 9 referenced -- zone determined and seismic check performed for central Uganda site

evals.prompt: Determine the design loads for a 25m simply supported bridge in the UK with a 7.3m carriageway. Provide the loading approach using current UK standards.
evals.expected_output: References EN 1991-2 (Eurocode 1 Part 2) with UK National Annex. Uses Load Model LM1 (UDL + tandem system per lane) instead of HA/HB. Notes that HA/HB loading (from BS 5400) has been replaced by LM1-LM4 in the UK. Partial factors from EN 1990, not MoWT Table 1. No seismic loading for UK. Module flagged as knowledge-based.

assertions.text: References EN 1991-2 or Eurocode 1 Part 2 for traffic loads (not BS 5400 or MoWT Vol 4)
Identifies LM1 as the replacement for HA loading, with UDL + tandem system approach
Notes that HA/HB loading is withdrawn in UK, replaced by LM1-LM4
UK module identified as knowledge-based

evals.prompt: Determine the design loads for a 60m simply supported bridge in Uganda with an 11.0m carriageway. The bridge is designed for 45 units of HB loading. The site is in western Uganda near Kasese (seismic Zone 1). Calculate: (1) HA UDL for the 60m loaded length using the correct formula, (2) number of notional lanes, (3) HB total load for 45 units, (4) earth pressure surcharge for HB 45 units behind abutments, and (5) seismic design requirements for this site.
evals.expected_output: HA UDL for L = 60m (> 30m): W = 151 * (1/60)^0.475 = approximately 20.2 kN/m. 3 notional lanes (11.0m in 7.6-11.4m range). HB 45 units: 450 kN per axle, 1800 kN total. Earth pressure surcharge for HB 45 = 20 kN/m2 per Section 5.8.2. Kasese is Zone 1 (A = 0.15), SPC 3 -- full seismic analysis required per Section 9. Response modification factors from Table 9.4 apply.

assertions.text: HA UDL calculated using formula W = 151*(1/L)^0.475 for L > 30m, result in range 19-22 kN/m (approximately 20.2 kN/m for L=60m)
Notional lane count is 3 (for 11.0m carriageway in 7.6-11.4m range)
HB 45 units total load in range 1700-1900 kN (45 * 10 * 4 = 1800 kN)
Earth pressure surcharge for HB 45 units identified as 20 kN/m2 per Section 5.8.2
Seismic design required -- site identified as Zone 1 (A = 0.15), SPC 3, with reference to Table 9.1/9.2 and full Section 9 analysis

evals.prompt: Determine the design traffic loading for a 25m span bridge on a Class B road in Kenya. The carriageway width is 7.3m. Apply Kenya RDM 4.2 EN/Eurocode loading. Calculate: (1) number of notional lanes, (2) LM1 UDL and tandem system loads with adjustment factors, (3) STR Set B factored loads using Tables 3.32 partial factors, and (4) check whether seismic design is needed for a central Kenya location.
evals.expected_output: Uses LM1 (not HA/HB). 2 notional lanes from Table 3.2 (7.3m carriageway). Kenya NDP adjustment factors from Table 3.3 applied: alpha_Q1=1.0, alpha_q1=0.61 for Lane 1. EN 1990 load combinations from Table 3.32 (STR Set B) with gamma_G=1.35 and gamma_Q=1.35. Uses gamma_G/gamma_Q notation (not gamma_fL). Gazette Notice 13048 referenced for Eurocode adoption. Seismic zone determined from Figure 3.30 for central Kenya.

assertions.text: Uses LM1 load model (not HA/HB loading) -- LM1 with UDL + tandem system per lane
Determines notional lanes from Table 3.2 -- 2 lanes for 7.3m carriageway
Applies Kenya NDP adjustment factors from Table 3.3 (alpha_q1=0.61 for Lane 1 UDL)
Uses EN 1990 load combinations from Tables 3.31-3.35 (not BS 5400 Table 1 combinations)
Uses gamma_G/gamma_Q notation (not gamma_fL) throughout
References Gazette Notice 13048 or notes Eurocode adoption in Kenya

evals.prompt: A bridge is being designed at the Ethiopia-Kenya border crossing. The bridge must accommodate traffic from both countries. Compare the ERA and Kenya RDM bridge loading standards. What load combinations, live load models, and seismic requirements would apply? Which standard should govern the design?
evals.expected_output: ERA bridge loading from ERA Bridge Design Manual. Kenya bridge loading from RDM. Comparison of live load models, load combinations, and seismic provisions. ERA has specific seismic zone requirements for Ethiopia. Design should use the more conservative loading from either standard. Notes ERA seismic zones vs Kenya seismic provisions.

assertions.text: References ERA Bridge Design Manual for Ethiopian loading standards
References Kenya RDM for Kenyan loading standards
Compares live load models between ERA and Kenya standards
Addresses Ethiopian seismic zone requirements from ERA Bridge Design Manual
Recommends using the more conservative standard for the border bridge design

evals.prompt: Determine the design loads for a 25 m span simply supported bridge on an ERA DC7 trunk road in Ethiopia. The bridge is in Seismic Zone 3 (Rift Valley margins, acceleration coefficient A = 0.12). Using the ERA Bridge Design Manual 2013, specify the HL-93 live load model, dynamic load allowance, load combinations for Strength I and Extreme Event I (seismic), and the applicable load factors.
evals.expected_output: Per ERA BDM 2013 Part 1 Chapter 3: HL-93 live load -- design truck (35+145+145 kN) or design tandem (2x110 kN) plus design lane load (9.3 kN/m). Dynamic load allowance IM = 33% for design truck/tandem. Load combinations: Strength I (gamma_DC=1.25, gamma_LL=1.75), Extreme Event I (gamma_EQ=1.0, gamma_LL varies). Seismic Zone 3 (A=0.12): single-mode spectral or uniform load analysis required. R factors applied per BDM Section 3.10.

assertions.text: References ERA BDM 2013 for bridge loading (not Kenya EN/Eurocode or Uganda BS 5400)
Uses HL-93 live load model with design truck (325 kN) and design lane load (9.3 kN/m)
Specifies dynamic load allowance IM = 33% per ERA BDM
Addresses Seismic Zone 3 requirements with A = 0.12 from ERA BDM Section 3.10
Provides LRFD load combination format (Strength I, Extreme Event I) with load factors
